identity.name: Cloud App — Okta Risky OAuth Drive-Scope Grant (T1550.001)
identity.summary: An OAuth authorize request is sent to an Okta org authorize endpoint (/oauth2/v1/authorize) carrying a drive-wide third-party scope. Cortex Cloud App Security tags the broad scope on the okta_sso audit event, the in-path NGFW EAL flags the outbound /authorize request shape, and an analytics rule recognises three risky-grant attempts inside 60s as a consent-phishing enumeration burst.
identity.description: OAuth application-consent phishing is the dominant initial-access vector against SaaS-first enterprises: an attacker registers a third-party app, lures a user to the IdP authorize endpoint, and harvests a long-lived access/refresh token instead of a password — bypassing MFA entirely. This scenario drives the oauth_grant_emulator plugin against an Okta org authorize endpoint with the risky_drive scope preset, which plants a drive-wide third-party scope (okta.users.read.self plus a broad files/Drive scope) into the authorize request. The detection approach is twofold: Cortex Cloud App Security parses the okta_sso OAuth grant audit event and flags any third-party (non-first-party client_id) app that requests a drive/files-wide scope, while a Cortex XSIAM analytics layer correlates repeated risky-grant attempts from one user/source into an enumeration-burst signal. The NGFW EAL plane independently sees the outbound HTTPS /oauth2/v1/authorize request to *.okta.com and contributes a network-side corroboration. A correlation rule fuses the CASB grant detection and the burst detection into one OAuth-consent-phishing incident. All requests carry a bogus canary client_id and resolve against a canary tenant, so no real token is ever issued — detection is on the outbound request and audit-event shape, not on a successful grant.

techniques.name: Use Alternate Authentication Material: Application Access Token

prerequisites.description: oauth_grant_emulator EAL plugin registered and Cortex Cloud App Security ingesting Okta system-log / OAuth grant audit events.
prerequisites.check_command: python3 -m scripts.eal_simulator.cli describe oauth_grant_emulator | jq -e '.eal_targets | length > 0'

prerequisites.description: Outbound HTTPS egress to the canary Okta authorize endpoint passes through the in-path NGFW EAL stack.

payload.code:
# Drive-wide third-party OAuth grant against the canary Okta authorize endpoint — bogus client_id, no token issued.
python3 -m scripts.eal_simulator.cli run /tmp/cloud-001.yml --live
# Repeat x3 within 60s to exercise the enumeration-burst analytics.
python3 -m scripts.eal_simulator.cli run /tmp/cloud-001-burst.yml --live

expected_artifacts.description: Okta OAuth grant audit event for a third-party app requesting a drive-wide scope.
expected_artifacts.expected_value: dataset = okta_sso and consent_type = "user" and oauth_scopes contains "drive"

expected_artifacts.description: Outbound HTTPS GET to the Okta /oauth2/v1/authorize endpoint carrying the scope query param.

biocs.name: CLOUD-001 Pre-Flight oauth_grant_emulator Plugin Registered
biocs.description: Pre-flight: oauth_grant_emulator metadata visible to operator. Confirms the EAL plugin is registered and its eal_targets enumerate the Cloud App OAuth-grant detections before any grant is emitted.
biocs.logic:
dataset = saas_audit_logs
| filter event_type = "eal_plugin_describe" or action contains "oauth_grant_emulator"
| filter app_name = "oauth_grant_emulator" or client_id = "oauth_grant_emulator"
| fields _time, user, app_name, action

xql_queries.name: CLOUD-001 Okta Risky Drive-Wide OAuth Scope Grant
xql_queries.query:
dataset = okta_sso
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter consent_type = "user"
| filter oauth_scopes contains_any ("drive", "Files.ReadWrite.All", "okta.users.read")
| filter client_id != null and client_id not contains "first_party"
| fields _time, user, app_name, client_id, oauth_scopes, consent_type, source_ip, geo

xql_queries.name: CLOUD-001 NGFW Okta OAuth Authorize Risky-Scope Egress
xql_queries.query:
dataset = saas_audit_logs
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter app_name = "okta-oauth" or dst_host contains ".okta.com"
| filter uri_path contains "/oauth2/v1/authorize"
| filter oauth_scopes contains_any ("drive", "Files.ReadWrite.All")
| fields _time, user, dst_host, uri_path, oauth_scopes, source_ip

xql_queries.name: CLOUD-001 Okta Repeated Risky Grant Enumeration Burst
xql_queries.query:
dataset = okta_sso
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter consent_type = "user"
| filter oauth_scopes contains_any ("drive", "Files.ReadWrite.All")
| comp count() as grant_attempts by user, source_ip, app_name
| filter grant_attempts >= 3
| fields user, source_ip, app_name, grant_attempts

correlation_rules.name: Okta Risky OAuth Drive-Scope Consent Phishing — Same Identity
correlation_rules.logic: XQL(CLOUD-001 Okta Risky Drive-Wide OAuth Scope Grant) OR XQL(CLOUD-001 NGFW Okta OAuth Authorize Risky-Scope Egress) OR XQL(CLOUD-001 Okta Repeated Risky Grant Enumeration Burst)

analytics_modules: Cortex Cloud App Security — Third-party app requests drive-wide OAuth scope
Cortex XSIAM Analytics — Repeated risky OAuth consent attempts from one identity

products.evidence_query: dataset = okta_sso | filter oauth_scopes contains "drive" and consent_type = "user" | fields _time, user, app_name, oauth_scopes
Correlation CR-CLOUDAPP-0001 fuses the CASB risky-scope grant, NGFW authorize egress, and enumeration burst into one consent-phishing incident.

use_cases.name: Risky OAuth Scope Grant Detection
use_cases.description: Detect a third-party app soliciting a drive-wide OAuth scope at the Okta authorize endpoint and correlate repeated attempts into a consent-phishing incident.

test_cases.objective: A single risky_drive grant fires the Cloud App risky-scope detection.
preconditions: oauth_grant_emulator registered and Okta audit logs ingested.
Detections from this card imported and enabled.
steps: Observe the CASB risky-scope alert and confirm the validation XQL returns >= 1 row.
success_criteria: XQL `CLOUD-001 Okta Risky Drive-Wide OAuth Scope Grant` returns >= 1 row.
NGFW egress validation query returns >= 1 row.

test_cases.objective: Three risky grants in <60s stitch into one enumeration-burst incident.
success_criteria: XQL `CLOUD-001 Okta Repeated Risky Grant Enumeration Burst` returns >= 1 row.

preventive_controls: Restrict OAuth app consent to admin-approved apps; disable end-user consent for third-party apps requesting drive/files scopes.
Maintain an allowlist of vetted first-party client_ids and block unverified publishers.
Require admin review for any app requesting broad data-access scopes.
detection_engineering: Alert on any third-party client_id requesting drive/files-wide scopes via the IdP audit log.
Aggregate risky-grant attempts per user/source to catch enumeration bursts.
Corroborate the audit event with the outbound /authorize request seen at the NGFW.

references.title: Unit42 - OAuth Phishing Apps in the Enterprise
MITRE ATT&CK T1550.001 — Application Access Token
